payload.description: Design and implement a live terminal view for open/in_progress tasks with compact hierarchy, blockers, and quick situational awareness.
payload.title: Feature: tsq watch live view for active tasks

payload.text:
[UX Plan: tsq watch live view]

Design direction
- Personality: Precision & Density + Utility & Function.
- Visual tone: monochrome-first with semantic status accents only.
- Goal: instant scan of active work (open + in_progress), blockers, and ownership in one stable terminal screen.

Problem and users
- Primary users: coding agents and developers coordinating multiple active tasks.
- Job to be done: answer "what is active now, what is blocked, what changed" without rerunning list commands.
- Success criteria:
  1) first actionable item identifiable in <3 seconds,
  2) blocked items obvious without reading full details,
  3) output remains readable at 80/100/120 column terminals.

Scope and constraints
- Command target: `tsq watch`.
- V1 scope: single repo, single user, local polling (no daemon).
- Default content scope: status in open|in_progress only.
- Non-goal: interactive editing/mutation from watch view in V1.
- Deterministic ordering: same ordering as list path (priority then created_at then id) unless explicitly sorted.

Information architecture (single-screen)
1) Header line: repo/task store + timestamp + refresh interval + active filters.
2) Summary line: total active, in_progress, open, blocked-by-deps count.
3) Main table/tree region: active tasks with compact metadata.
4) Footer hint line: key hints and how to exit.

Data fields per row
- Required: status glyph+label, id, title, priority, assignee.
- Conditional: parent marker, blocker count, dependency hints.
- Optional expansion lane (when width allows): blockers list and dependents list.

State model
- Loading: clear indicator "loading..." in main region.
- Healthy refresh: timestamp updates each cycle.
- No active tasks: explicit empty state "no active tasks".
- Error transient (IO/lock/read): keep last good frame + inline error banner.
- Error persistent: banner escalates severity after N consecutive failures.

Accessibility/legibility
- Never rely on color alone; keep glyph + status text always present.
- No-color fallback must preserve all semantics.
- Keep IDs and status left-anchored for rapid scan.
- Ensure copy/paste stability (plain text, no cursor-control required for fallback mode).

Layout and responsive behavior
- Wide (>=120): single-line primary rows + inline meta flow.
- Medium (90-119): primary row + second meta lane for blockers/dependents.
- Narrow (<90): truncated title, always show status+id; move meta to wrapped lines.

ASCII wireframe
[tsq watch] repo=tasque  refreshed=2026-02-18T01:30:00Z  interval=2s  filter=status:open,in_progress
active=7  in_progress=3  open=4  blocked=2
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
◐ in_progress tsq-41267b.2 Implement: tsq stale command + service query [p1 @SG] {blocks-on: tsq-41267b.2.1}
○ open        tsq-41267b.3.1 Decision: list filter contract [p1]
○ open        tsq-41267b.6   Feature: tsq watch live view for active tasks [p1]
  {unblocks: tsq-41267b.6.1,tsq-41267b.6.2}
...
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
q quit | r refresh-now | p pause/resume | h help

Interaction model (V1)
- Non-interactive mutations; watch is read-only.
- Keys:
  - q: quit,
  - r: force immediate refresh,
  - p: pause/resume polling,
  - h: inline key help.
- If key handling is not available in given terminal/runtime, fallback to pure polling output mode.

Command contract proposal
- `tsq watch`
- `tsq watch --interval <seconds>` (default 2, min 1)
- `tsq watch --status <status>` (repeatable or comma list)
- `tsq watch --assignee <name>`
- `tsq watch --tree` (optional tree mode within watch)
- `tsq watch --once` (single frame render, no loop)
- `tsq watch --json` => newline-delimited frame objects for automation.

JSON frame shape (watch --json)
- `{ schema_version, command:"tsq watch", ok:true, data:{ frame_ts, interval_s, filters, summary, tasks } }`
- summary includes: total, open, in_progress, blocked.

Performance and reliability
- Polling loop should avoid overlapping refreshes (skip if previous cycle still running).
- On errors, emit non-fatal frame with `ok:false` while retaining process unless fatal setup error.
- Use existing read path (`loadProjectedState`) to preserve durability semantics.

Acceptance criteria for implementation task
1) default watch shows only open/in_progress,
2) refresh loop stable for 10+ minutes without memory growth issues,
3) deterministic ordering preserved across frames,
4) clear empty/error states,
5) snapshot tests for 80/100/120-width frame rendering.

payload.description: Prevent loss of detailed implementation/design specs by attaching canonical specs to tasks, validating integrity, and gating implementation workflows.
payload.title: Feature: spec durability workflow (attach/check/claim gate)
Decision: task schema extensions for spec metadata + integrity fingerprint
Decision: .tasque/specs structured storage layout and lifecycle rules
Design: tsq spec attach/check command contracts and JSON envelopes
Implement: spec attach flow writes canonical file under .tasque/specs
Implement: spec check validation (required sections + fingerprint drift)
Implement: claim/start guard with --require-spec policy
Tests/docs: spec workflow e2e, edge cases, and migration notes

payload.text:
[Spec durability schema and storage proposal]

Goal
- Make full task specs first-class durable artifacts.
- Ensure specs are discoverable, validated, and referenced from task records.

Schema updates (task model)
- Add optional task-level fields:
  - spec_path?: string            # canonical location under .tasque/specs
  - spec_fingerprint?: string     # sha256 of canonical spec content
  - spec_attached_at?: string     # ISO timestamp
  - spec_attached_by?: string     # actor
  - spec_version?: number         # schema for spec metadata (start at 1)
  - spec_status?: "attached"|"missing"|"drifted"|"invalid"

Event model changes
- Add event types:
  - task.spec_attached
  - task.spec_checked
- task.spec_attached payload:
  - spec_path, spec_fingerprint, spec_attached_at, spec_attached_by, spec_version
- task.spec_checked payload:
  - spec_path, expected_fingerprint, observed_fingerprint, status, checked_at, checker

Projection behavior
- task.spec_attached updates task spec metadata and marks spec_status=attached.
- task.spec_checked updates spec_status to attached|drifted|invalid|missing.
- Keep append-only audit history; never mutate history in-place.

JSON output impact
- show/list/search JSON includes spec_* fields when present.
- `tsq spec check --json` returns:
  - task_id, spec_path, status, expected_fingerprint, observed_fingerprint, missing_sections[]

[Structured storage under .tasque]

Canonical storage layout
- Root: .tasque/specs/
- Per-task directory: .tasque/specs/<task-id>/
- Files:
  - spec.md                 # canonical full spec document
  - metadata.json           # optional generated metadata cache
  - history/<timestamp>.md  # optional retained snapshots (if --keep-history)

Why per-task directory
- Stable discoverability by task ID.
- Easy copy/move/archive with task lifecycle.
- Supports future attachments without path churn.

Attach flow (`tsq spec attach <id> <source>`)
1) Resolve task ID.
2) Read source (file path only in V1).
3) Validate minimum structure headings.
4) Write canonical `.tasque/specs/<id>/spec.md` atomically.
5) Compute sha256 fingerprint on canonical content.
6) Append `task.spec_attached` event with metadata.
7) Return JSON envelope with path + fingerprint.

Check flow (`tsq spec check <id>`)
1) Resolve task and expected metadata.
2) Load canonical spec path.
3) Recompute fingerprint.
4) Validate required sections.
5) Append `task.spec_checked` event.
6) Return status and diagnostics.

Required sections (V1)
- Overview
- Constraints / Non-goals
- Interfaces (CLI/API)
- Data model / schema changes
- Acceptance criteria
- Test plan

[Command surface and policy]

Commands
- tsq spec attach <id> <source-path>
  - --force                # overwrite canonical spec and write new attach event
  - --print-path           # output canonical path only
- tsq spec check <id>
  - --strict               # exit non-zero if missing section or fingerprint drift
- tsq spec show <id>       # print canonical spec path + summary metadata

Claim/start policy
- Extend update claim flow with optional gate:
  - tsq update <id> --claim --require-spec
- Behavior with --require-spec:
  - fail if no spec_path/fingerprint
  - fail if check status is missing/drifted/invalid
  - pass only when current canonical spec hash matches expected fingerprint and sections valid

Exit codes
- 0 success
- 1 validation/user error
- 2 IO/storage error
- 4 spec integrity/policy gate failure (new)

Planning defaults
- Make spec attach/check optional globally in V1, strict per-command via --require-spec.
- Keep schema backward compatible for tasks without spec metadata.

payload.title: Refactor skill installer to copy SKILLS/<name> source directory
Bypass init wizard when explicit skill action flags are passed
Add release bump script and document GitHub release workflow
Add seamless release flow gated by package.json version sync
Rust port of Bun app (1:1 compatibility)
Audit TS behavior and define Rust spec
Define Rust crate layout + dependencies
Port storage + events + lock + snapshots
Port domain + service logic
Port CLI + rendering + watch
Port skill management + init wizard
Rust parity tests and verification

payload.text: Rust app service parity landed: added TasqueService with split write modules, added Rust skills installer/uninstaller module, and cargo check passes for src-rust.
Core Rust skills operation module is now ported (install/uninstall logic in src-rust/src/skills). Remaining: wire CLI init wizard and command flags to TasqueService::init.
Parallel sub-agent execution started for Rust CLI/render/watch parity and compile unblock.
Parallel sub-agent execution started for Rust parity test port and verification.

Completed Rust CLI/render/watch port with functional parity updates: strict CSV validation for list filters, watch non-fatal refresh loop + TTY interactive controls, and full cargo check/test passing.
Completed Rust parity verification suite: watch/list/search/json envelope/init parity tests added and passing (21 integration tests total).
Completed init wizard + skill management parity in Rust; fixed wizard invalid-target re-prompt behavior; added init parity regression tests; cargo check/test passing.
Child features .5/.6/.7 are closed with Rust src-rust CLI/service/tests ported and verified via cargo check/test.

payload.title: Default TUI v1 (read-only watch + kanban)

payload.text: Started implementation: UX design completed; coding split across worker sub-agents (TUI module/tests + CLI routing).

Implemented Rust TUI v1: no-arg TTY opens list view, Tab switches list/board kanban, read-only inspector, explicit 	ui subcommand, and parity tests in tests/tui_once_parity.rs. Verified with cargo fmt/clippy/tests + PTY smoke.
Added docs/tui-v1-design.md with v1 interaction/layout scope and entry/fallback rules.

payload.title: TUI v2 full redesign design doc

payload.text: Delivered full UX redesign spec in docs/tui-v2-redesign.md (precision-density direction, IA/layout, components, interaction matrix, tokens, accessibility).
Updated v2 redesign doc to explicitly surface spec attachment state in Task Table, Board cards, Inspector, interaction matrix, color tokens, and copy guidelines.

payload.title: Implement TUI spec attachment indicators

payload.text: Started implementation with parallel tracks: TUI rendering changes + regression tests for spec indicators.

payload.title: Incorporate Beads-style views into TUI v2 spec

payload.text: Updated docs/tui-v2-redesign.md to match provided reference style with Tasque-native tabs (Tasks/Epics/Board), dense pill tables, epic progress header, and 3-column board; removed Beads as a view label.
Started full TUI redesign implementation in parallel: tui layout/views refactor + --epics CLI flag + updated regression tests.

Implemented redesigned TUI shell: Tasks/Epics/Board tabs, dense table columns (ID/Type/Title/Status/Assignee/Priority/Spec), epic progress header, 3-column board (Open/In Progress/Done), --epics flag, and updated spec indicator rendering/tests.

payload.title: Migrate TUI runtime to OpenTUI (Bun)

payload.text: Started OpenTUI migration: scaffolded tui-opentui via create-tui (react) and wiring Rust interactive launch path.

Fixed OpenTUI loader warning on Windows: list does not accept CSV/repeated --status. Loader now uses single --status when only one status is selected, otherwise fetches list and filters statuses client-side.
OpenTUI update: made table columns responsive (hide Spec/Assignee before wrapping) and added Tree/Deps tabs. Deps tab uses tsq dep tree --json for selected task.

Added Tree and Deps tabs in OpenTUI. Tree shows parent/child hierarchy; Deps tab fetches and renders tsq dep tree for selected task. Also fixed narrow-width wrapping by adaptive columns hiding Spec/Assignee.
Inspector UX: auto-hide when entering compact width (<120 cols), toggle with i on all widths, render as bottom panel on compact when visible, and side panel on wider terminals.
Replaced text status indicators with compact status icons across Tasks/Epics table, Board cards, Tree rows, Deps lines, and Inspector. Kept color semantics and reduced status column to icon marker.
Removed separate status + number columns. Status icon now renders immediately left of task ID across Tasks, Epics, Tree, Board (ID line), Deps tree lines, and Inspector.
Added keyboard filter cycling with f. Presets include custom/env, open, in_progress, closed, canceled, done (closed+canceled), and full (all). Filtering now applied client-side across all views.

Fixed vertical overflow squishing header: pinned header/tab/footer + inspector with flexShrink=0, constrained main pane minHeight=0, and capped Board/Deps rows with overflow indicator so chrome stays stable.
Removed assignee from list columns and switched kind rendering from bracketed tokens to proper labels (Task/Feature/Epic) across rows, board cards, and inspector.
Polishing OpenTUI header information hierarchy and table alignment spacing (status/icon + spec column width consistency).
Combining Tasks and Tree: Tasks tab will render hierarchical tree rows to improve scanability; removing separate Tree tab path.
Implemented combined Tasks+Tree UX: Tasks tab now renders hierarchical tree rows; standalone Tree tab removed from navigation and key map (views now 1/2/3/4).

Applied UX type-ramp/spacing pass: Tasks tree rows now 2-line (title + metadata: priority/planning/spec/updated), inspector removed, and Enter opens attached spec in modal dialog with keyboard scrolling.

Refined task tree typography: removed section title, bold task title, metadata line with pipes and human labels (Needs planning / No Spec / Updated : DD Mon YY), root full IDs and child suffix IDs (.1/.2.1).

Adjusted tasks tree detail row per feedback: no section title, bold title emphasis via text attributes, metadata as 'P# | Needs planning/Planned | No Spec/Spec Attached/Spec Invalid | Updated : DD Mon YY', and child IDs rendered as suffixes (.1/.2.1) while roots keep full IDs.
